steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject of the clause is singular: “the word 'lasso'”. The parenthetical “(a rope)” is descriptive and doesn't change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. The subject is "the word 'lasso'" — singular. The sentence states a general fact (etymology), so simple present is appropriate. Rule: a singular subject takes a third-person singular verb (adds -s).
  • Choice A — "were deriving": Plural past progressive — wrong in number, tense, and aspect. Etymology is a standing fact, not a past ongoing action. ✗
  • Choice B — "have derived": Plural present perfect — wrong number and the perfect tense implies a completed change, not a permanent origin. ✗
  • Choice C — "derive": Plural base form — wrong number for a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "derives": Singular simple present. Correctly agrees with the singular subject "the word 'lasso'" and states a permanent fact. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
